Hey guys, JP here, the wolf is in the house, time through daily motivation slash skills training. This is kind of, neither of those, this is like a tactic. It's like actually a great truth about selling and that is the value of having good leads. You know, one thing I see when I go around the world, guys, especially when I train lots of sales bosses, they call me in and they want me to teach the straight line system, write their scripts, construct their loops, the whole nine yards, right? And coach people back and forth with tonality and by language if it's in person, right? But I would say 90% of the time that I do this, what I find also is that they're not supplying the sales force with the correct number or quality of leads. And if it's a situation where they're not supplying them at all and it's up to the salesman, which is perfectly fine, that happens many places, right? And the salesperson, you, is not investing enough time and energy and resources in getting good leads. Understand this, the best metaphor is like, you know, you could be this straight line expert which makes you the proverbial Ferrari capable of going 220 miles an hour, five seconds or three seconds of zero to 60 or monster, right? But what gives you the power? Gasoline, right? And the gasoline is the leads. So please don't scrimp on your leads. It's a cost of doing business, all right? You can buy leads, you can advertise for leads online, okay? You can work joint ventures with people to get them to mail to those. Whatever you are and by that I go through a ton of these different strategies and many of my different trainings and the intensives and also on the certification of course, right? The point is, is that don't put yourself in a situation where you possess all these great skills for closing, but you have no one to call. Like for instance, with my sales force right now, I'm starting them purposely giving them not bad leads, but very rough unqualified leads that were kind of old and almost dead. Why? Because I want them to get the cobwebs out. I want them to get the marbles out of their mouth where they're learning and growing. They're just starting. So I started it, but they're about to approach the point now where I'm launching the certification program and now that they're really primed, they get it because I made them struggle with Bill's muscle. Now I'm going to make sure these guys are women too are hooked up with leads because without that they're just spinning their wheels. You can call out of the phone book or you could get qualified leads coming in from marketing and the latter is so much more effective. Just remember, listen, maybe you're in a situation where they provide you, maybe not. Either way, you got to realize here that it's an integral part of success. So if you're not getting them from the company you work for, not enough quantity of them, equality, think entrepreneurially. Every dollar you invest in yourself in terms of your lead gen, it's going to come back to you 10 fold as long as you're doing it right in terms of your marketing. So you don't waste your money, but if you're doing it right, you should get a five, 10 to one return. And also remember, it's not always what you make on the opening sale. It's the life of the customer. So very often, yeah, you might break even on the initial sale, but now you go about building a customer for life and that's massive success. All right. Just remember, don't ignore this one crucial aspect of being a world class close, which is having a supplier leads. At Stratton, I would have top salesmen, they would pay out of their pockets, that's three and four cold callers, just cold on the low and providing them with leads. Because why would they want to spend their time cold calling? It's a low hit ratio. Their animal closers, they want someone who's going to just give them qualified leads. They can close the ones who are already interested. In the purest form of a salesperson, you're only speaking to people who are interested at pre-qualified, which means you're speaking to people who are hopefully buyers of heat and buyers of power. Now, no matter how great your program is, even if you have someone filtering out the bad ones, they always tend to wrap what we call wooden leads, meaning they're bad leads. They're not real. And those will be your lucky losing and your mistakes. They slip through. So your job as a salesperson, as a world-class salesperson, is to filter those out. You want to separate out the bad ones from the good ones and move those first two categories of buyers and power buyers. And you move those two down the straight line from the open to the close and just have massive quantity of them. Don't put yourself in a position where you're scarce for leads. It's going to really trip you up. All right? I love you. And we'll talk again tomorrow. And tomorrow's lesson will be on tonality. Talk to them. See you. Talk to them.
